preamble.summary: Restoration promises and the Davidic covenant. THIS CHAPTER CONTAINS THE SINGLE MOST THEOLOGICALLY SIGNIFICANT LXX OMISSION IN ALL OF JEREMIAH. MT vv. 14-26 — THIRTEEN FULL VERSES containing the promise of the Righteous Branch, the perpetual Davidic covenant, the perpetual Levitical covenant, and the covenant with day and night — are ENTIRELY ABSENT from LXX. These are among the most important messianic texts in Jeremiah.
preamble.notable_variants: LXX LACKS vv. 14-26 entirely. This is a block of 13 verses containing: (a) the Righteous Branch prophecy (vv. 14-16, paralleling 23:5-6), (b) the promise that David shall never lack a man on the throne (v. 17), (c) the promise that the Levitical priests shall never lack a man to offer (v. 18), (d) the covenant with day and night as guarantee of David's line (vv. 19-22), and (e) the affirmation that the 'two families' chosen will not be rejected (vv. 23-26). This massive omission may indicate: (1) these verses were a later MT expansion elaborating on 23:5-6, or (2) the LXX Vorlage simply lacked them. The material partially duplicates 23:5-6, which IS present in LXX, supporting the expansion theory. This is one of the most debated textual issues in all of Jeremiah.
preamble.structural_notes: MT ch. 33 = LXX ch. 40. LXX ch. 40 has only 13 verses (= MT 33:1-13). MT 33:14-26 has NO LXX equivalent whatsoever.

verses.mt_reading: הִנֵּה יָמִים בָּאִים נְאֻם־יְהוָה וַהֲקִמֹתִי אֶת־הַדָּבָר הַטּוֹב אֲשֶׁר דִּבַּרְתִּי אֶל־בֵּית יִשְׂרָאֵל וְעַל־בֵּית יְהוּדָה
verses.mt_rendering: Behold, the days are coming, says the LORD, when I will fulfill the good word which I spoke concerning the house of Israel and the house of Judah.
variant_notes: ENTIRELY ABSENT from LXX. This begins the 13-verse messianic expansion (vv. 14-26) not found in the Greek. This verse introduces the fulfillment of the 'good word' and launches the Righteous Branch prophecy.

verses.mt_reading: בַּיָּמִים הָהֵם וּבָעֵת הַהִיא אַצְמִיחַ לְדָוִד צֶמַח צְדָקָה וְעָשָׂה מִשְׁפָּט וּצְדָקָה בָּאָרֶץ
verses.mt_rendering: In those days and at that time I will cause a Righteous Branch to sprout for David, and He shall execute justice and righteousness in the land.
variant_notes: ABSENT from LXX. This is the Righteous Branch prophecy, closely paralleling 23:5. The phrase צֶמַח צְדָקָה 'Branch of Righteousness' is a key messianic title. Its absence from LXX here (while present at 23:5 in LXX) suggests this is a secondary duplication in MT.

verses.mt_reading: בַּיָּמִים הָהֵם תִּוָּשַׁע יְהוּדָה וִירוּשָׁלִַם תִּשְׁכֹּן לָבֶטַח וְזֶה אֲשֶׁר־יִקְרָא־לָהּ יְהוָה צִדְקֵנוּ
verses.mt_rendering: In those days Judah shall be saved and Jerusalem shall dwell safely; and this is the name by which she shall be called: 'The LORD our Righteousness.'
variant_notes: ABSENT from LXX. Note the difference from 23:6 — there the Branch is named 'The LORD our Righteousness' (masculine); here JERUSALEM is given the name (feminine). This theological distinction exists only in MT.

verses.mt_reading: כִּי כֹה אָמַר יְהוָה לֹא־יִכָּרֵת לְדָוִד אִישׁ יֹשֵׁב עַל־כִּסֵּא בֵּית יִשְׂרָאֵל
verses.mt_rendering: For thus says the LORD: David shall never lack a man to sit upon the throne of the house of Israel.
variant_notes: ABSENT from LXX. The perpetual Davidic throne promise — a cornerstone of messianic theology — is not in the Greek tradition of Jeremiah 33. It IS expressed elsewhere (2 Sam 7, Ps 89, etc.).

verses.mt_reading: וְלַכֹּהֲנִים הַלְוִיִּם לֹא־יִכָּרֵת אִישׁ מִלְּפָנַי מַעֲלֶה עוֹלָה וּמַקְטִיר מִנְחָה וְעֹשֶׂה זֶבַח כָּל־הַיָּמִים
verses.mt_rendering: And the Levitical priests shall not lack a man before Me to offer burnt offerings, to burn grain offerings, and to make sacrifices continually.
variant_notes: ABSENT from LXX. The perpetual Levitical priesthood promise is paired with the Davidic promise. Both dynasties — royal and priestly — are guaranteed.

verses.mt_rendering: And the word of the LORD came to Jeremiah, saying:
variant_notes: ABSENT from LXX.

verses.mt_reading: כֹּה אָמַר יְהוָה אִם־תָּפֵרוּ אֶת־בְּרִיתִי הַיּוֹם וְאֶת־בְּרִיתִי הַלָּיְלָה וּלְבִלְתִּי הֱיוֹת יוֹמָם וָלַיְלָה בְּעִתָּם
verses.mt_rendering: Thus says the LORD: If you can break My covenant with the day and My covenant with the night, so that there should not be day and night in their season —
variant_notes: ABSENT from LXX. The 'covenant with day and night' is used as an unbreakable guarantee of the Davidic line. This cosmic-covenant argument is unique to this passage.

verses.mt_reading: גַּם בְּרִיתִי תֻפַר אֶת־דָּוִד עַבְדִּי מִהְיוֹת־לוֹ בֵן מֹלֵךְ עַל־כִּסְאוֹ וְאֶת־הַלְוִיִּם הַכֹּהֲנִים מְשָׁרְתָי
verses.mt_rendering: — then also My covenant may be broken with David My servant, so that he should not have a son to reign upon his throne, and with the Levites the priests, My ministers.
variant_notes: ABSENT from LXX.

verses.mt_reading: אֲשֶׁר לֹא־יִסָּפֵר צְבָא הַשָּׁמַיִם וְלֹא יִמַּד חוֹל הַיָּם כֵּן אַרְבֶּה אֶת־זֶרַע דָּוִד עַבְדִּי וְאֶת־הַלְוִיִּם מְשָׁרְתֵי אֹתִי
verses.mt_rendering: As the host of heaven cannot be numbered, nor the sand of the sea measured, so I will multiply the seed of David My servant, and the Levites who minister to Me.
variant_notes: ABSENT from LXX. The Abrahamic 'stars and sand' imagery applied to the Davidic line.

verses.mt_rendering: And the word of the LORD came to Jeremiah, saying:
variant_notes: ABSENT from LXX.

verses.mt_reading: הֲלוֹא רָאִיתָ מָה הָעָם הַזֶּה דִּבְּרוּ לֵאמֹר שְׁתֵּי הַמִּשְׁפָּחוֹת אֲשֶׁר בָּחַר יְהוָה בָּהֶם וַיִּמְאָסֵם
verses.mt_rendering: Have you not considered what this people have spoken, saying, 'The two families which the LORD chose — He has rejected them'?
variant_notes: ABSENT from LXX. The 'two families' (royal and priestly, or Israel and Judah) are referenced by their detractors.

verses.mt_reading: כֹּה אָמַר יְהוָה אִם לֹא בְרִיתִי יוֹמָם וָלָיְלָה חֻקּוֹת שָׁמַיִם וָאָרֶץ לֹא שָׂמְתִּי
verses.mt_rendering: Thus says the LORD: If My covenant is not with day and night, if I have not appointed the ordinances of heaven and earth —
variant_notes: ABSENT from LXX.

verses.mt_reading: גַּם זֶרַע יַעֲקוֹב וְדָוִד עַבְדִּי אֶמְאַס מִקַּחַת מִזַּרְעוֹ מֹשְׁלִים אֶל־זֶרַע אַבְרָהָם יִשְׂחָק וְיַעֲקֹב כִּי אָשִׁיב אֶת־שְׁבוּתָם וְרִחַמְתִּים
verses.mt_rendering: — then I will also reject the seed of Jacob and David My servant, so that I will not take of his seed to be rulers over the seed of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. For I will restore their captivity and have compassion on them.
variant_notes: ABSENT from LXX. This concluding verse ties the Davidic promise to the patriarchal promises (Abraham, Isaac, Jacob) and ends with a restoration promise. The entire 13-verse block (14-26) is the LARGEST continuous MT-only passage in Jeremiah and one of the most significant textual differences in the entire Hebrew Bible.
